entries.title: OSI Model (7 Layers)
entries.content: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application. TCP/IP stack maps roughly to layers 1–4 and 7.

entries.title: TCP vs UDP
entries.content: TCP: reliable, ordered, connection-oriented (web, email). UDP: fast, no guarantee (streaming, gaming, DNS).

entries.title: IP Address
entries.content: IPv4: 32-bit (e.g. 192.168.1.1). IPv6: 128-bit for more addresses. DHCP assigns IPs dynamically on networks.

entries.title: DNS
entries.content: Domain Name System translates names (google.com) to IP addresses. Uses hierarchical servers and caching (TTL).

entries.title: HTTP Status Codes
entries.content: 200 OK, 301 redirect, 400 bad request, 401 unauthorized, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 500 server error.
